page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-32-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4a server family. It is configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and a total memory capacity of 256.0 GB. This allocation provides a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include any GPU accelerators, and the local storage size is listed as none, although the basic description references six local SSDs.

From a resource density perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance class suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is driven by its high memory density, allowing organizations to run large in-memory databases or caching layers without paying for excess compute cores.

This server type is designed for workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale data caching, enterprise applications, and memory-heavy data processing pipelines. The lack of GPU hardware makes it unsuitable for deep learning training, while the dedicated x86_64 architecture provides broad compatibility for standard enterprise software stacks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-32-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server within the c4a family, configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. This setup yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation for predictable performance and does not include GPU accelerators. While the local storage size is officially designated as none, the basic configuration details reference six local SSDs. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications, offering high memory density relative to its compute allocation. It is well-suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, large caching layers, enterprise application hosting, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that do not require graphics acceleration.
